etymology.description: 禾(禾) (hé, "wheat; grain") + 人(人) (rén) = originally 'harvest' -> semantic shift to 'year'
etymology.interpretation: The character 年 originally combined 禾(禾) (hé, "wheat; grain") as a semantic element suggesting 'harvest' with 人(人) (rén) as a phonetic component. This reflects the agricultural foundation of the concept of a 'year,' marking the cyclical nature of harvests. Over time, the original form evolved through various scripts, converging in the clerical script era to its current form with standardization.

examples.chinese: 今年是幸福的一年(今年是幸福的一年)。
examples.pinyin: Jīnnián shì xìngfú de yī nián.
examples.english: This year is a happy year.

examples.chinese: 每一年都有新希望(每一年都有新希望)，我们努力迎接挑战(我們努力迎接挑戰)。
examples.pinyin: Měi yī nián dōu yǒu xīn xīwàng, wǒmen nǔlì yíngjiē tiǎozhàn.
examples.english: Every year holds new hope, and we strive to embrace challenges.
